Home / Productivity Score Calculator

Productivity Score Calculator

Rate your solopreneur productivity with a scored assessment. Get actionable tips based on your deep work hours, tool stack, and meeting load.

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your total weekly deep work hours (uninterrupted, focused work).
2
Count how many tools/apps you actively use each week.
3
Enter your average number of meetings per week.
4
Review your productivity score and grade.
5
Read your wins and action items for specific improvements.
6
tools.solopreneur-productivity-score.how_to_use.5

Frequently Asked Questions

What counts as deep work?

Deep work = focused, uninterrupted time on your most cognitively demanding tasks (coding, writing, strategy, design). No email, Slack, social media, or phone. 50+ minute blocks minimum.

What is a good productivity score?

70+ is solid. 85+ is excellent. The score is diagnostic — use it to identify your weakest area and improve it over 2-4 weeks.

How many tools should an entrepreneur use?

3-5 core tools is the sweet spot. Every additional tool adds cognitive overhead. Audit quarterly: if you haven't used a tool in 2 weeks, cancel it.

Are all meetings bad?

No — customer calls, sales demos, and 1:1s with contractors are high-value. It is internal status meetings and "quick syncs" that kill productivity. Batch them.

How often should I recalculate my score?

Monthly. Track your deep work hours and meeting count for a week, then recalculate. Small changes compound — adding 5 hours of deep work per week is 260 more hours per year.

Results

Copy All
Example

📈 Productivity Score: 75/100

Grade: B — Solid Systems 📊 Breakdown: • Weekly Deep Work: 15 hours ⚡ • Tools in Stack: 5 tools ✅ • Weekly Meetings: 3 meetings ⚡ ✅ WINS: • Perfect tool stack size. Enough to automate, not so many that you drown in subscriptions. 💡 ACTION ITEMS: • Aim for 15-20 hours of deep work per week. Block 3-4 hour morning sessions. • Try to batch all meetings on one day (e.g., Thursday). This keeps the rest of the week meeting-free. 🎯 Your #1 lever: You are on track. Protect your current systems and incrementally improve. 📌 Score Formula: Base 50 + Deep Work (max +25) + Tool Stack (max +15) + Meeting Efficiency (max +20). Max: 100. Min: 10.
